risks: HNS-enabled storage (ADLS Gen2) is set as the ML workspace default storage account, which is not supported by Azure Machine Learning
Standard_LRS on the storage account provides no geo-redundancy; a single datacenter failure could cause data loss for production ML training data
Public network access is enabled on the ML workspace, exposing training endpoints and data to the public internet
No managed identity configured for the ML workspace; best practices require managed identity for secure access to associated resources
No resource tags defined on any resource, reducing governance and cost-tracking visibility
correctionsRecommended: Create a separate non-HNS storage account for the ML workspace default datastore and keep the HNS-enabled storage as a linked data lake for training data
Upgrade the storage account SKU to Standard_GRS or Standard_ZRS for production-grade data resilience
Configure a system-assigned or user-assigned managed identity on the ML workspace for secure, keyless access to Key Vault, Storage, and Container Registry
Add private endpoints and disable public network access on the ML workspace for production security
Add consistent resource tags (e.g., environment, project, costCenter) to all resources
hardDependencies: ML workspace default storage account (stmltrainingprod001) has isHnsEnabled=true, which is incompatible with Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces — Azure ML does not support hierarchical namespace on the default storage account
